prompt: Add a GraphQL endpoint using Strawberry

turns.content:
<task>
Add a GraphQL endpoint using the Strawberry library for product queries.
</task>

arguments.query: strawberry-graphql Python Flask integration query type example

turns.content:
1. Strawberry GraphQL - https://strawberry.rocks/docs
   pip install strawberry-graphql[flask]. Type-safe GraphQL with Python dataclasses.
2. Strawberry Flask integration - https://strawberry.rocks/docs/integrations/flask
   Use GraphQLView.as_view().

arguments.title: Strawberry GraphQL setup

turns.content:
1	 from sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, String, Float
2	 from database import Base
3	 
4	 class Product(Base):
5	     __tablename__ = 'products'
6	     id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
7	     name = Column(String(200))
8	     price = Column(Float)
9	     category = Column(String(100))

1	 from flask import Flask
2	 from api.routes import api_bp
3	 
4	 app = Flask(__name__)
5	 app.register_blueprint(api_bp)

arguments.content:
from flask import Flask
from strawberry.flask.views import GraphQLView
from api.routes import api_bp
from graphql_schema import schema

app = Flask(__name__)
app.register_blueprint(api_bp)
app.add_url_rule('/graphql', view_func=GraphQLView.as_view(schema=schema))

arguments.message:
GraphQL endpoint is live at /graphql with Strawberry's built-in GraphiQL IDE. You can query products with optional category filter:
```graphql
query { products(category: "electronics", limit: 10) { id name price } }
```

arguments.summary: Added GraphQL endpoint using Strawberry. Product type with queries for list (with category filter) and single product lookup. GraphiQL IDE included.
arguments.final_answer: GraphQL endpoint at /graphql using Strawberry. Supports products query with optional category filter and single product lookup by ID. Includes GraphiQL IDE for development.
